question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request-level caching in accountRepository to send the same /account/balance request repeatedly with identical parameters, causing the same four DB queries to execute on every call with no memoization, generating sustained DB load that raises resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user alternate between alias-based and numeric accountId formats for the same account in rapid succession, causing getCryptoEntity to alternate between selectCryptoEntityByAlias (UNION ALL with entity_history) and selectCryptoEntityById (UNION ALL with entity_history), preventing any query plan caching benefit and sustaining high DB CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a consensusEnd timestamp that is just before a partition boundary, causing selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound to return the second-to-last partition's lower bound, which may be very old, forcing latestBalanceBeforeConsensus to scan a very wide time range in account_balance and increasing I/O above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user send /account/balance requests for many different valid account aliases in parallel, each triggering the full four-query chain in RetrieveBalanceAtBlock, causing the DB connection pool to be saturated and CPU to spike above 30% without any per-IP or per-account rate limiting at the persistence layer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: selectCryptoEntityByAlias (SQL)] Can an unprivileged external user provide a consensusEnd value that is very old (near genesis), causing the timestamp_range @> @consensus_end check in selectCryptoEntityByAlias to match a large number of historical entity rows in entity_history, forcing a large intermediate result set to be sorted by ORDER BY timestamp_range DESC, increasing memory and CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getBalanceChange()] Can an unprivileged external user identify an account with a very high transaction volume and repeatedly query its balance at the current block tip, causing balanceChangeBetween to SUM over thousands of crypto_transfer rows on every request, generating sustained aggregation load that raises node resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RetrieveBalanceAtBlock does not deduplicate or batch requests, sending N concurrent requests for the same account and consensusEnd, causing N×4 identical DB queries to execute simultaneously, multiplying DB load and raising resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: latestBalanceBeforeConsensus (SQL)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a treasury_entity_id-equivalent account as the target, causing the outer subquery in latestBalanceBeforeConsensus (selecting from account_balance where account_id = @treasury_entity_id) to scan a high-volume account's balance history, and the inner correlated subquery to execute for each matching row, creating a multiplicative query cost that raises CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a consensusEnd of int64 max value, causing getInclusiveInt8Range(consensusEnd, consensusEnd) to produce a range that matches every row in entity and entity_history where timestamp_range @> [max, max], potentially causing unexpected full-table scans if the range operator is not efficiently indexed for extreme values?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user craft requests targeting accounts that were deleted and recreated multiple times, causing getCryptoEntity to return a result from entity_history after scanning many historical rows, and then causing getLatestBalanceSnapshot and getBalanceChange to each execute expensive range queries, cumulatively raising resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a consensusEnd timestamp that is between two partition boundaries where no account_balance snapshot exists for the target account, causing latestBalanceBeforeConsensus to scan the full range between lower_bound and timestamp without finding a match, performing a full partition scan before returning a zero balance, increasing I/O above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: selectCryptoEntityByAlias (SQL)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ORDER BY timestamp_range DESC in selectCryptoEntityByAlias (which has no LIMIT clause) to force the DB to sort the entire UNION ALL result set in memory before returning the first row, causing memory pressure and CPU spikes above 30% when entity_history contains many rows for the queried alias?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user send /account/balance requests with a blockIdentifier pointing to the genesis block (consensusEnd near 0), causing getLatestBalanceSnapshot to find no partition lower bound and scan the entire account_balance table, while getBalanceChange scans all crypto_transfer rows from timestamp 0, maximizing per-request DB cost and raising resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with alias-based accountIds that match zero rows in entity but many rows in entity_history, causing the UNION ALL in selectCryptoEntityByAlias to fully scan entity_history for the alias + timestamp_range combination, returning a large intermediate result set that must be sorted, increasing memory and CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getBalanceChange()] Can an unprivileged external user identify an account that is the recipient of many small transfers (e.g., a popular smart contract or exchange account) and query its balance at a block far from the last snapshot, causing balanceChangeBetween to SUM over tens of thousands of crypto_transfer rows, generating a high-cost aggregation that raises DB CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of result caching between getCryptoEntity, getLatestBalanceSnapshot, and getBalanceChange to send requests where each sub-query hits a different DB partition, preventing buffer cache reuse and causing repeated cold reads from disk that raise I/O above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound (SQL)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a consensusEnd timestamp that is very large (near int64 max), causing the CTE in selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound to scan all rows in mirror_node_time_partitions where from_timestamp <= @timestamp (potentially all rows), performing a full table scan before applying ORDER BY + LIMIT 2, increasing CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for accounts with alias-based IDs at historical consensusEnd timestamps, causing getCryptoEntity to execute the more expensive selectCryptoEntityByAlias (UNION ALL + ORDER BY) instead of selectCryptoEntityById, and then triggering getLatestBalanceSnapshot with a wide partition range, compounding the per-request cost to raise resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getLatestBalanceSnapshot()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-query structure of getLatestBalanceSnapshot (first selectPreviousPartitionLowerBound, then latestBalanceBeforeConsensus) to cause two expensive DB round-trips per balance lookup, and by sending many concurrent requests, saturate the DB connection pool and raise resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with numeric accountIds that have many entries in entity_history (e.g., accounts that were updated many times), causing selectCryptoEntityById to scan all entity_history rows for that id with timestamp_range @> consensusEnd, sort them by timestamp_range DESC, and apply LIMIT 1, with the sort being the expensive step when entity_history is large?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user send /account/balance requests with no blockIdentifier (triggering RetrieveLatest) followed by requests with specific historical blockIdentifiers, causing the system to alternate between current-state and historical queries, preventing DB query plan caching and increasing CPU above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: balanceChangeBetween (SQL)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request where the errata filter (errata is null or errata <> 'DELETE') in balanceChangeBetween forces a full scan of the crypto_transfer table without using an index on the errata column, causing a filter-heavy aggregation that raises CPU above 30% when the table is large?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: RetrieveBalanceAtBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests for a high-volume account (e.g., one involved in many token transfers) at a consensusEnd just after a balance snapshot, causing getBalanceChange to aggregate only a small number of transfers but getLatestBalanceSnapshot to scan a wide partition range, creating an asymmetric cost profile that is hard to detect but cumulatively raises resource consumption above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/persistence/account.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getCryptoEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with alias-based accountIds where the alias is a valid but rare byte sequence, causing selectCryptoEntityByAlias to perform a full scan of entity_history (if no index exists on alias for historical records) before returning zero results, and by repeating this with many different rare aliases, sustain high DB I/O above 30%?
